channel.title: Recruitment (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Recruitment" - For PF to succeed, we're going to need a whole bunch of people with a wide variety of skill sets. If you want to contribute, please sign up in the appropriate recruitment thread(s).

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Software Support, Systems Administration, or Quality Assurance.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

If you feel you have these skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with Sysadmin, QA, or Support and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with these skills. >
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: <Self Explanatory>
  • What technologies are you familiar with in your area? For instance, do you use Jira to track QA issues? Do you work with JUnit for Unit tests? Ever used Docker? Tell me about your abilities here:

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. I'm super awesome at bash. I write all my own scripts, and I'm a l33t h@xx0r…
A. That is….cool. What I need are people who are versed in toolsets, administrative practices, and skills of that variety. If it was just writing scripts, that'd be more the programming section. Systems Administration and QA (while not perfectly in sync) are very different skill sets from programmers usually. I don't want to confuse the two.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Artists and Graphic Design
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.
  5. Any art work produced for PF will have to be licensed as part of the Site. You're probably going to just let us use it with credit to you as the artist.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Artists.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

If you feel you have these skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Please give me a link to your DeviantArt, or your tumblr, wheverever:

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. I drew this kickass darkblade once, and it had like katanas and…
A. We're looking for people who are going to be able to create assets for our site. Logos, backgrounds, etc. If you're making fan art, kick ass, you do you. This is a little different.

Q. So…what, you're just asking if Zhange or Sunny is willing to help?
A. No. Lots of people who make digital assets are proficient in graphic design rather than traditional "art". (Though I would love to have Zhange or Sunny on the teeeeam <3 :D)

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Marketing and Fundraising
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Marketing, financial, or budgetary skills.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

If you feel you have these skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with marketing, or budget issues and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with marketing. >
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: <Self Explanatory>
  • Please provide any kind of example or qualification you feel would demonstrate your qualifications in this area:

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. Okay, so I have this idea for a marketing campaign…
A. That's awesome. Feel free to pitch it in the appropriate phase.

Q. I own my own business, does that qualify me for this?
A. Yes! If you own your own business, and manage the running of it, that's valuable experience we will probably need when it comes to the day-to-day running of something.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Legal and Risk
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Legal or Risk Management skills.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

DISCLAIMER
This is a skill which is going to require proof of ability. If you are not a professional, I do not suggest you apply to this section.

If you feel you have risk management or legal skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with legal issues and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with legal or risk management. >
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: <Self Explanatory>
  • Please explain your professional qualifications here:

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. Do I have 'professional experience' if I'm a legal student?
A. Maybe. You should already be able to answer the question "can I contribute to PF in a legal or risk context" before responding. If the answer is "maybe" or "no" reconsider.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Management
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Management skills.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

DISCLAIMER
This is a skill which is going to require proof of ability. Management is a responsibility, not a power. If you volunteer yourself for this, you will be under significant scrutiny, and will have high expectations placed on you.

If you feel you have management skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with management and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with management. Not "I've lead a group project in class", but what you've done as a professional, as a job, for compensation. This will let us know who to assign as potential management staff, or more difficult tasks. Please do not mislead us about your skill levels.>
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for management: <Self Explanatory>
  • Please answer the follow question, and PM me your answer, along with an example of a team or project that you've managed: You have two team members who aren't completing a task on time. Both of them promised they would have the work done, but one of them dissapeared for two weeks and didn't turn in anything, while the other messaged you every few days, telling you they'll have it done, then produced nothing after the deadline. What do you do?

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. Wait, when you say manager, you mean I get to tell people what to do?!
A. Kind of. I need people who I can delegate the unenviable task of managing people to. This means you're 1) Beholden to your manager as well, and 2) will be expected to be responsive, and respectful to those you manage. Managers aren't "The boss"; their job is to remove obstacles, and provide tasks.

Q. If I'm a manager, does that make me tech staff?
A. No. The technical staff are people who are on the tech team who I feel have the potential, temperament, and skillset to be authority on the wiki. Project Foundation is a completely separate entity from the idea of being technical staff.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Technical Skills
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Programming and software development.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

If you feel you have skills as a software developer, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with software development and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with Programming and software development. Not "this one summer I wrote a webpage", but what you've done as a professional, as a job, for compensation. This will let us know who to assign as potential management staff, or more difficult tasks. Please do not mislead us about your skill levels.>
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications: <Self Explanatory>
  • Language proficiencies <What software do you know how to write?>

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. I wrote an HTML page once, can I be a programmer?
A. We're looking for people who have existing and concrete skills in this area. As much as I appreciate your enthusiasm, it's more important that we find any and everyone who has major skills in this area.

Q. What languages are you looking for?
A. We're not really concerned with which language yet, that's not been decided. I am, however, interested in what your skills are.
